sections.title: 1. Purpose
sections.section_prompt: Draft a purpose statement explaining that this document captures the documented design input requirements for {device_name_model} per ISO 13485:2016 §7.3.3, including functional, performance, usability, regulatory, and risk-control inputs. Reference {ref:RD-001} as the governing plan and {ref:RD-007} as the source of risk-derived inputs.

sections.title: 2. Scope
sections.content: Applies to all design inputs for {device_name_model} manufactured by {company_legal_name}. Inputs are traced forward to design outputs ({ref:RD-003}), verification ({ref:RD-005}), and validation ({ref:RD-006}).

sections.title: 3. Intended Use & User Profile
4. Design Input Categories (ISO 13485 §7.3.3)
clauses: 4.1 Functional, performance, usability and safety requirements according to intended use
4.2 Applicable statutory and regulatory requirements (EU MDR / US FDA QSR / national)
4.4 Information derived from previous similar designs, where applicable
4.5 Other requirements essential for design and development
sections.section_prompt: For each clause above, write 2–4 sentences describing how {company_legal_name} satisfies the requirement for {device_name_model}. Be specific and audit-ready; reference the corresponding record in the requirements register table that follows.

sections.title: 5. Design Input Requirements Register
sections.ai_rows_prompt: Generate 12–18 realistic, traceable design input requirements for {device_name_model} (intended use: {intended_use}). Cover all five clause categories (functional, performance, usability/IEC 62366, safety/risk-derived, regulatory, biocompatibility/sterility if applicable, software/cybersecurity if applicable). Each row: req_id like REQ-001 sequential; category one of Functional|Performance|Usability|Safety|Regulatory|Software|Interoperability; requirement is a single SHALL statement; source cites the originating document or standard (e.g. ISO 14971 hazard H-04, EU MDR Annex I 14.2, user need UN-03); acceptance is measurable and verifiable. Return JSON array.

sections.title: 6. Regulatory & Standards Inputs
sections.ai_rows_prompt: List 6–10 standards and regulations applicable to {device_name_model} given the markets selected. Always include ISO 13485:2016 and ISO 14971:2019. Add IEC 62366-1 if device has user interface, IEC 62304 if software, IEC 60601-1 if electrical medical equipment, ISO 10993-1 if patient contact, EU MDR 2017/745 if EU market, 21 CFR 820 if US market, UKCA / MHRA if UK. Return JSON array.

sections.title: 7. Traceability
sections.content: Each requirement in this document is traced through the Requirements Traceability Matrix maintained as part of the Design History File ({ref:RD-009}). Forward links to design outputs ({ref:RD-003}), verification protocols ({ref:RD-005}), and validation activities ({ref:RD-006}) shall be kept current at each design review ({ref:RD-004}).
